nodes.label: Layered DAG Architecture Constraint
nodes.rationale: The layered DAG (Architecture Â§1073 + Â§1052) enforces that higher-level modules (engine, dispatcher, cli) cannot be imported by lower-level foundation modules (errors, ids, contracts, config, concurrency). This prevents circular dependencies and enforces separation of concerns. ADR-010 encodes these rules. Known gaps include adopt->cli widening and dashboard read-only semantics which are not expressible at import-graph level.

nodes.label: noqa:secret Escape Hatch Pattern
nodes.rationale: Every suppression of a hardcoded-secret lint warning must carry an audit trail via '# noqa: secret -- <reason>' of at least 10 characters. Plain '# noqa: secret' without reason is itself a violation, ensuring suppressions are traceable and deliberate (NFR-SEC-1).

nodes.label: Specialist Cross-Ref Validation Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Architecture Concern #15 defines the specialist validation pipeline (Â§1043). The real pipeline (Story 2A-2) will parse agents/index.yaml, validate YAML frontmatter against SpecialistFrontmatter Pydantic contract, and cross-reference skill/workflow/command IDs against workflow YAMLs. Currently a placeholder exiting 0.

nodes.label: POSIX-Only flock Design Decision
nodes.rationale: The file locking module uses fcntl.flock which is POSIX-only (Architecture Â§573). The async path offloads blocking flock calls via asyncio.to_thread (Architecture Â§727, Decision B2) to avoid blocking the event loop. Windows users receive ImportError at module import time. The concurrency/__init__.py conditionally imports locks only on non-Windows platforms.

nodes.label: Env Var Allow-List Security Design
nodes.rationale: Architecture Â§671 + NFR-SEC-2 require that env var reads are restricted to SDLC_*, CLAUDE_* prefixes and the exact token GH_TOKEN (for pr-author specialist). Forbidden reads raise ConfigError immediately, preventing accidental credential leakage through unrestricted os.environ access.

nodes.label: Five-Wire Format Pydantic Contracts
nodes.rationale: All five wire-format contracts (JournalEntry, ResumeToken, HookPayload, SpecialistFrontmatter, WorkflowSpec) use Pydantic BaseModel with extra='forbid', frozen=True, and strict schema_version validators. The frozen+extra-forbid pattern enforces immutability and schema stability across the five-wire architecture (Story 1.7).

nodes.label: Kebab-Case Slug Validation Pattern
nodes.rationale: All identifier slugs must match lowercase kebab-case (^[a-z0-9]+(?:-[a-z0-9]+)*$). IDs follow the hierarchical pattern EPIC-<slug>-S<NN>-<slug>-T<NN>-<slug>. The private _SLUG_RE is shared between parsers.py and builders.py (intra-module import allowed by boundary rules). Numbers are zero-padded to 2 digits with max value 99.
